lede: A look at December 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kurt Waldheim and Masaccio, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Bethlehem
events.desc: The city of Bethlehem passes from Israeli to Palestinian control.

events.title: Netherlands
events.desc: A Dutch DC-10, flight Martinair MP 495, crashes at Faro Airport, Portugal, killing 56.

events.title: Pan Am Flight 103
events.desc: A bomb explodes on board Pan Am Flight 103 over Lockerbie, Dumfries and Galloway, Scotland, killing 270. This is to date the deadliest air disaster to occur on British soil.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: A Thai-Japanese Pact of Alliance is signed.

events.title: Lieutenant Kijé (Prokofiev)
events.desc: Lieutenant Kijé, one of Sergei Prokofiev's best-known works, premiered.

events.title: Nepal
events.desc: United Kingdom and Nepal formally sign an agreement of friendship, called the Nepal–Britain Treaty of 1923, which superseded the Treaty of Sugauli signed in 1816.

events.title: Anarchism
events.desc: American anarchist Emma Goldman is deported to Russia.

events.title: Nora Helmer
events.desc: the fictional character of Nora Helmer is first portrayed by Betty Hennings at a showing of A Doll's House at the Royal Danish Theatre.

events.title: Rochdale Society of Equitable Pioneers
events.desc: The Rochdale Society of Equitable Pioneers commences business at its cooperative in Rochdale, England, starting the Cooperative movement.

events.title: Ryazan
events.desc: The city of Ryazan is sacked by the Mongol army of Batu Khan.

births.name: Kurt Waldheim
births.line: Austrian colonel and politician; 9th President of Austria

births.name: Hu Jintao
births.line: Chinese engineer and politician, 5th Paramount leader of China

births.name: Konstantin Rokossovsky
births.line: Marshal of the Soviet Union during World War II

deaths.text: Albert King, American singer-songwriter, guitarist, and producer
